title: Ensure App Service Environment is provisioned with v3 or  higher

input_row.title: Ensure App Service Environment is provisioned with v3 or  higher
input_row.description: Ensure App Service Environment is provisioned with v3 or higher to benefit from the latest enhancements.
input_row.rationale: Older versions of App Service Environment require manual management of Azure resources and have greater scaling limitations.
input_row.audit: Audit from Azure Portal 1. Go to App Service Environments. 2. Click the name of an App Service Environment. 3. Ensure that Version displays App Service Environment v3 or higher. 4. Repeat steps 1-3 for each App Service Environment. Audit from Azure CLI Run the following command to list App Service Environments: az appservice ase list For each App Service Environment, ensure that kind is set to ASEV3 or higher. Audit from Azure Policy If referencing a digital copy of this Benchmark, clicking a Policy ID will open a link to the associated Policy definition in Azure. If referencing a printed copy, you can search Policy IDs from this URL: https://portal.azure.com/#view/Microsoft_Azure_Policy/PolicyMenuBlade/~/Definitions • Policy ID: eb4d34ab-0929-491c-bbf3-61e13da19f9a - Name: 'App Service Environment should be provisioned with latest versions'
input_row.remediation: Remediate from Azure Portal When creating an App Service Environment from the portal, after clicking + Add, ensure that Create App Service Environment v3 is displayed.
input_row.impact: Manual configuration may be required to complete the migration and to optimize your App Service plan SKU choice to meet your needs.
input_row.default_value: The default version is currently App Service Environment v3.
input_row.additional_information: App Service Environment v1 and v2 were retired as of 31 August, 2024. As of September 1, 2024, Azure auto-migrated any remaining App Service Environment v1 and v2 on a best-effort basis.
input_row.justification: The control pertains specifically to Azure App Service Environments, which do not have a direct equivalent in AWS services. The first AI opinion incorrectly attempted to apply AWS App Runner services to this Azure-specific control. The second AI opinion correctly identified the mismatch and highlighted that the control is not applicable to AWS services. Therefore, this control cannot be automated using AWS APIs, as it is outside the scope of AWS.
input_row.manual_summary: 1. Access the Azure Portal and navigate to the "App Service Environments" section. 2. Click on each App Service Environment to view its details. 3. Verify that the "Version" field displays "App Service Environment v3" or higher. 4. Repeat the above steps for each App Service Environment to ensure compliance.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**PROGRAM NAME:** azure_app_service_environment_version_check

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
1. Use Azure CLI command `az appservice ase list` to list all App Service Environments.
2. Query the output to check the `kind` property of each App Service Environment.
3. Verify that the `kind` is set to `ASEV3` or higher.
4. Clear Pass/Fail criteria:
   - Pass: If all App Service Environments have `kind` set to `ASEV3` or higher.
   - Fail: If any App Service Environment has a `kind` set below `ASEV3`.
5. If utilizing Azure SDK for Python, you can use `Azure App Service Management Client` to enumerate App Service Environments and confirm their versions by checking the `kind` attribute.
